 245\t    C --> PA\n   246\t    PA --> AD\n   247\t    AD --> PR\n   248\t    PR --> R\n   249\t    R --> O\n   250\t```\n   251\t\n   252\tThe key boundary is that the orchestrator uses the skill to understand what should happen, then uses the CLI to make one bounded thing happen, then writes the result into its own durable state. The CLI does not own orchestration lifecycle between calls.\n   253\t\n   254\t### Manifest and Build Metadata\n   255\t\n   256\tThe build surface needs one new manifest-level concept: a bundled runtime artifact that belongs inside the packaged skill even though it does not belong inside the composed markdown body.\n   257\t\n   258\tProposed shape:\n   259\t\n   260\t```json\n   261\t{\n   262\t  \"skills\": {\n   263\t    \"ls-claude-impl\": {\n   264\t      \"name\": \"ls-claude-impl\",\n   265\t      \"phases\": [\"claude-impl\"],\n   266\t      \"references\": [\n   267\t        \"claude-impl-reading-journey\",\n   268\t        \"claude-impl-process-playbook\",\n   269\t        \"claude-impl-cli-operations\",\n   270\t        \"claude-impl-prompt-system\"\n   271\t      ],\n   272\t      \"bundledArtifacts\": [\n   273\t        {\n   274\t          \"source\": \"dist/generated/ls-impl-cli.cjs\",\n   275\t          \"destination\": \"bin/ls-impl-cli.cjs\"\n   276\t        }\n   277\t      ]\n   278\t    }\n   279\t  }\n   280\t}\n   281\t```\n   282\t\n   283\tThe manifest key name can change, but the build contract should not: one explicit source artifact copied into one explicit destination inside the packaged skill.\n   284\t\n   285\t## Dependency and Version Grounding\n   286\t\n   287\tThe runtime should stay intentionally small. The command surface needs argument parsing and subcommands. The config layer needs explicit file loading and schema validation. Everything else can start with built-in Node/Bun APIs until a real pain point justifies another abstraction.\n   288\t\n   289\t### Stack Additions\n   290\t\n   291\t| Package | Version | Purpose | Research Confirmed |\n   292\t|---|---:|---|---|\n   293\t| `citty` | `0.2.2` | CLI builder with subcommands, help generation, and lazy command loading | Yes — current latest stable on npm and aligns well with this feature’s command model |\n   294\t| `c12` | `3.3.4` | Explicit config-file loading for orchestrator-owned JSON config | Yes — pin the latest stable 3.x line for v1; npm `latest` currently points at `4.0.0-beta.4`, which is intentionally avoided |\n   295\t| `zod` | `4.3.6` | Schema validation for run config and operation results | Yes — current latest stable on npm |\n   296\t| `typescript` | `6.0.3` | Typecheck/runtime authoring for process code and build scripts | Yes — current latest stable on npm |\n   297\t| `@types/node` | `22.19.17` | Node-compatible type surface aligned with the chosen runtime floor | Yes — pinned to the Node 22 target line instead of the latest current line |\n   298\t\n   299\t### Packages Considered and Rejected\n   300\t\n   301\t| Package | Decision | Rationale |\n   302\t|---|---|---|\n   303\t| `c12@4.0.0-beta.4` | Rejected for v1 | Beta release. Stable 3.x is sufficient for one explicit config file. |\n   304\t| `execa@9.6.1` | Deferred | Helpful API, but not necessary until raw child-process management becomes painful. |\n   305\t| `tinyexec@1.1.1` | Deferred | Nice minimal wrapper, but built-in Node APIs are sufficient for initial provider adapters. |\n   306\t| `tsup@8.5.0` | Rejected for v1 | Bun bundler already supports Node target and single-file bundling needs for the packaged artifact. |\n   307\t\n   308\t### Runtime and Packaging Decisions\n   309\t\n   310\t- **Authoring runtime:** TypeScript in `processes/impl-cli/`\n   311\t- **Distribution target:** Node-compatible bundled JS\n   312\t- **Minimum Node runtime:** `>=22`\n   313\t- **Minimum Bun runtime:** `>=1.3.12`\n   314\t- **Build tool:** Bun bundler\n   315\t- **Bundled asset strategy:** prompt markdown assets embedded into a generated TS module before bundle time\n   316\t- **Config format:** explicit `impl-run.config.json` at the spec-pack root\n   317\t- **`package.json` engines:** add `\"node\": \">=22\"` and a matching Bun expectation in developer docs/CI\n   318\t\n   319\t## Verification Scripts\n   320\t\n   321\tThis repo already uses `bun run verify` as its primary project gate. The new runtime feature adds implementation code, so the project needs explicit verification tiers before story work begins.\n   322\t\n   323\t| Script | Purpose | When Used | Composition |\n   324\t|---|---|---|---|\n   325\t| `red-verify` | Red-phase gate | after writing tests, before Green | `bunx tsc --noEmit && bun run build && bun run validate` |\n   326\t| `verify` | Standard development gate | routine implementation | `bun run red-verify && bun test` |\n   327\t| `green-verify` | Green-phase gate | after implementation is green | `bun run verify && bun run guard:no-test-changes` |\n   328\t| `verify-all` | Deep verification and release gate | story completion, pre-release | `bun run green-verify && bun run smoke:impl-cli` |\n   329\t\n   330\t### Canonical Gate Mapping\n   331\t\n   332\tFor this repo, the default mapping should be:\n   333\t\n   334\t| Gate | Command |\n   335\t|---|---|\n   336\t| Story gate | `bun run green-verify` |\n   337\t| Epic gate | `bun run verify-all` |\n   338\t\n   339\tIf a project-specific override exists, preflight should surface it explicitly with source metadata. If not, these are the defaults the orchestrator records.\n   340\t\n   341\tSupporting scripts to add:\n   342\t\n   343\t- `typecheck`: `tsc --noEmit`\n   344\t- `guard:no-test-changes`: fail if test files changed during Green\n   345\t- `smoke:impl-cli`: run a bundled-CLI smoke test against a minimal fixture spec pack\n   346\t\n   347\tIf no deeper integration smoke exists yet, `smoke:impl-cli` may emit a visible skip notice in its earliest version, but it must not silently pass as if a real smoke already ran.\n   348\t\n   349\t## Work Breakdown Summary\n   350\t\n   351\tThe implementation work breaks into six chunks. Chunk 0 establishes the shared foundation. The remaining chunks align to coherent vertical capabilities and track cleanly to the epic’s story breakdown without forcing a brittle one-to-one mapping.\n   352\t\n   353\t| Chunk | Scope | ACs Covered |\n   354\t|---|---|---|\n   355\t| Chunk 0 | Foundation: config schema, result contracts, prompt asset embedding, packaged CLI build path, fixture skeletons | supports all downstream chunks |\n   356\t| Chunk 1 | Skill surface: `SKILL.md`, progressive disclosure references, manifest/build integration for packaged runtime | AC-1.3 to AC-1.6, AC-3.4, AC-6.1 to AC-6.3 |\n   357\t| Chunk 2 | Spec-pack inspection and preflight: layout validation, run-config validation, provider availability checks, insert detection | AC-1.1 to AC-1.6, AC-2.1 to AC-2.4 |\n   358\t| Chunk 3 | Prompt system and story implementation runtime: prompt assembly, implementor launch, continuation, self-review loop, implementor result schema | AC-3.1 to AC-3.4, AC-4.1 to AC-4.5 |\n   359\t| Chunk 4 | Story verification and fix routing: verifier batch, quick fix, disagreement handling, story gate support | AC-5.1 to AC-5.5, AC-6.1 to AC-6.3 |\n   360\t| Chunk 5 | Epic cleanup, verification, synthesis, and packaged runtime smoke | AC-7.1 to AC-7.3, AC-8.1 to AC-8.4 |\n   361\t\n   362\t### Chunk Dependencies\n   363\t\n   364\t```text\n   365\tChunk 0\n   366\t  ↓\n   367\tChunk 1\n   368\t  ↓\n   369\tChunk 2\n   370\t  ↓\n   371\tChunk 3\n   372\t  ↓\n   373\tChunk 4\n   374\t  ↓\n   375\tChunk 5\n   376\t```\n   377\t\n   378\tChunk 1 can begin once the foundation contracts are in place, but Chunk 2 should not start until the skill surface and file layout are stable enough to support real fixture-based inspection/preflight tests.\n   379\t\n   380\t## Resolved Decisions\n   381\t\n   382\t| Decision | Resolution |\n   383\t|---|---|\n   384\t| Packaged runtime artifact format | Ship CommonJS in v1 at `bin/ls-impl-cli.cjs` |\n   385\t| Copilot `--npx` fallback | Defer from v1; support directly available Copilot CLI only |\n   386\t\n   387\t## Deferred Items\n   388\t\n   389\t| Item | Related AC | Reason Deferred | Future Work |\n   390\t|---|---|---|---|\n   391\t| Full `ls-team-impl-cc` style Claude-only fallback behaviors | AC-2.2, AC-2.3 | Epic explicitly allows a narrower Claude-only fallback for v1 | later Claude-only enhancement epic |\n   392\t| SDK extraction of `ls-impl-cli` | AC-4 through AC-8 | The public contract should support future extraction, but extraction itself is not part of this feature | future SDK epic |\n   393\t| Web app harness tools using the same operation contracts | AC-4 through AC-8 | Future consumer, not current deliverable | future harness epic |\n   394\t\n   395\t## Self-Review Notes\n   396\t\n   397\tThis revision closes the major open contract seams that were still too interpretive in the earlier draft:\n   398\t\n   399\t- Copilot is no longer treated as a retained implementor fallback in v1.\n   400\t- gate mapping is now explicit\n   401\t- envelope status/outcome/exit-code routing is now explicit\n   402\t- cleanup result and artifact persistence contracts are explicit\n   403\t- companion discovery and story ordering rules are explicit\n   404\t\n   405\t## Open Questions\n   406\t\n   407\t| # | Question | Owner | Blocks | Resolution |\n   408\t|---|---|---|---|---|\n   409\t| None | No unresolved design-time questions remain after this revision pass. | — | — | Closed |\n   410\t\n   411\t## Related Documentation\n   412\t\n   413\t- Epic: `01-claude-impl-cli-skill-epic.md`\n   414\t- Companion design: `tech-design-skill-process.md`\n   415\t- Companion design: `tech-design-cli-runtime.md`\n   416\t- Test plan: `test-plan.md`\n   417\t- Existing design notes: `docs/ls-claude-impl-design-notes.md`\n   418\t- Existing reference runtime: `processes/codex-impl/`\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
docs/spec-build/epics/01-claude-impl-cli-skill/artifacts/story-02/006-verifier-codex53-high.jsonl:12:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_5","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc 'nl -ba docs/spec-build/epics/01-claude-impl-cli-skill/tech-design-skill-process.md'","aggregated_output":"     1\t# Technical Design Companion: Skill and Process Surface\n     2\t\n     3\t## Purpose\n     4\t\n     5\tThis companion covers the orchestrator-facing side of `ls-claude-impl`: the markdown skill entrypoint, the progressive-disclosure references, the orchestrator-owned state artifacts, and the process playbook that tells Claude Code what to do and when to use the CLI. The goal of this surface is not to execute work directly. Its job is to minimize cognitive load for the orchestrator while still giving the orchestrator enough understanding to supervise the implementation process critically.\n     6\t\n     7\tThe orchestrator should never have to reverse-engineer the process from CLI help text or scattered repository notes. The skill surface should answer five questions cleanly:\n     8\t\n     9\t1. What artifacts must exist before a run can begin?\n    10\t2. What should the orchestrator read first, and what can wait?\n    11\t3. What state does the orchestrator own and update?\n    12\t4. Which CLI operation should be used next?\n    13\t5. What evidence is required before the orchestrator can progress?\n    14\t\n    15\t## Skill Surface Layout\n    16\t\n    17\tThe skill should not be a monolith. It should load in layers, with each deeper file answering the question raised by the previous layer.\n    18\t\n    19\t| File | Role in the Workflow | Why It Exists |\n    20\t|---|---|---|\n    21\t| `src/phases/claude-impl.md` | Startup orientation and setup contract | Keeps initial context short and action-oriented |\n    22\t| `src/references/claude-impl-reading-journey.md` | Post-setup reading order for the spec pack | Avoids loading the full artifact world before the orchestrator knows where to look |\n    23\t| `src/references/claude-impl-process-playbook.md` | Stage-by-stage process rules | Gives the orchestrator one durable place to learn transitions, receipts, and escalation behavior |\n    24\t| `src/references/claude-impl-cli-operations.md` | Public operation guide | Keeps command semantics explicit without bloating `SKILL.md` |\n    25\t| `src/references/claude-impl-prompt-system.md` | Prompt architecture and insert explanation | Explains the runtime prompt model at the process level without exposing provider-specific implementation details inline |\n    26\t\n    27\tThe orchestrator starts with `SKILL.md`, resolves the spec-pack location, performs the minimum initial checks, and only then reads the reading-journey file. That file tells the orchestrator what to read from the spec pack and what to extract into durable state before deeper runtime work begins.\n    28\t\n    29\t### Skill-Pack Layout in Dist Output\n    30\t\n    31\tThe packaged skill should land with this shape:\n    32\t\n    33\t```text\n    34\tdist/skills/ls-claude-impl/\n    35\t├── SKILL.md\n    36\t├── references/\n    37\t│   ├── claude-impl-reading-journey.md\n    38\t│   ├── claude-impl-process-playbook.md\n    39\t│   ├── claude-impl-cli-operations.md\n    40\t│   └── claude-impl-prompt-system.md\n    41\t└── bin/\n    42\t    └── ls-impl-cli.cjs\n    43\t```\n    44\t\n    45\tThe runtime file lives beside the docs, but the docs remain the authoritative explanation of the process. The orchestrator should be able to succeed by reading the docs and then using the CLI. The CLI should not have to teach the methodology.\n    46\t\n    47\t## Orchestrator-Owned Artifacts\n    48\t\n    49\tThe orchestrator is the state authority for a run. The CLI is not the owner of run lifecycle, progression, or recovery. That boundary is what keeps the future SDK/harness surface clean.\n    50\t\n    51\t### `impl-run.config.json`\n    52\t\n    53\t`impl-run.config.json` is the machine-readable declaration of the intended run shape. The orchestrator authors it or updates it during initialization. The CLI validates it before any provider-backed work begins.\n    54\t\n    55\t```json\n    56\t{\n    57\t  \"version\": 1,\n    58\t  \"primary_harness\": \"claude-code\",\n    59\t  \"story_implementor\": {\n    60\t    \"secondary_harness\": \"codex\",\n    61\t    \"model\": \"gpt-5.4\",\n    62\t    \"reasoning_effort\": \"high\"\n    63\t  },\n    64\t  \"quick_fixer\": {\n    65\t    \"secondary_harness\": \"codex\",\n    66\t    \"model\": \"gpt-5.4\",\n    67\t    \"reasoning_effort\": \"medium\"\n    68\t  },\n    69\t  \"story_verifier_1\": {\n    70\t    \"secondary_harness\": \"codex\",\n    71\t    \"model\": \"gpt-5.4\",\n    72\t    \"reasoning_effort\": \"xhigh\"\n    73\t  },\n    74\t  \"story_verifier_2\": {\n    75\t    \"secondary_harness\": \"none\",\n    76\t    \"model\": \"claude-sonnet\",\n    77\t    \"reasoning_effort\": \"high\"\n    78\t  },\n    79\t  \"self_review\": {\n    80\t    \"passes\": 3\n    81\t  },\n    82\t  \"epic_verifiers\": [\n    83\t    {\n    84\t      \"label\": \"epic-verifier-1\",\n    85\t      \"secondary_harness\": \"codex\",\n    86\t      \"model\": \"gpt-5.4\",\n    87\t      \"reasoning_effort\": \"xhigh\"\n    88\t    },\n    89\t    {\n    90\t      \"label\": \"epic-verifier-2\",\n    91\t      \"secondary_harness\": \"none\",\n    92\t      \"model\": \"claude-sonnet\",\n    93\t      \"reasoning_effort\": \"high\"\n    94\t    }\n    95\t  ],\n    96\t  \"epic_synthesizer\": {\n    97\t    \"secondary_harness\": \"codex\",\n    98\t    \"model\": \"gpt-5.4\",\n    99\t    \"reasoning_effort\": \"xhigh\"\n   100\t  }\n   101\t}\n   102\t```\n   103\t\n   104\tThe key simplicity rule is that the file should only say what the orchestrator actually needs to decide: the primary harness, the per-role secondary harness, the model selection, and the self-review pass count. It should not carry derived values, convenience aliases, or runtime details that the orchestrator does not need to reason about.\n   105\t\n   106\t### Default Resolution Algorithm\n   107\t\n   108\tThe orchestrator owns the config file, but it should not invent defaults ad hoc. The skill surface should teach one deterministic default-generation algorithm:\n   109\t\n   110\t1. Set `primary_harness` to `claude-code`.\n   111\t2. Probe secondary harness availability in this order:\n   112\t   - Codex available\n   113\t   - else Copilot available\n   114\t   - else no secondary harness\n   115\t3. If Codex is available, use Codex as the default secondary harness for all GPT-lane roles:\n   116\t   - story implementor: `codex`, `gpt-5.4`, `high`\n   117\t   - quick fixer: `codex`, `gpt-5.4`, `medium`\n   118\t   - story verifier 1: `codex`, `gpt-5.4`, `xhigh`\n   119\t   - story verifier 2: `none`, `claude-sonnet`, `high`\n   120\t   - epic verifier 1: `codex`, `gpt-5.4`, `xhigh`\n   121\t   - epic verifier 2: `none`, `claude-sonnet`, `high`\n   122\t   - epic synthesizer: `codex`, `gpt-5.4`, `xhigh`\n   123\t4. If Codex is unavailable but Copilot is available, use Copilot only for fresh-session roles:\n   124\t   - story implementor: `none`, `claude-sonnet`, `high`\n   125\t   - quick fixer: `copilot`, `gpt-5.4`, `medium`\n   126\t   - story verifier 1: `copilot`, `gpt-5.4`, `xhigh`\n   127\t   - story verifier 2: `none`, `claude-sonnet`, `high`\n   128\t   - epic verifier 1: `copilot`, `gpt-5.4`, `xhigh`\n   129\t   - epic verifier 2: `none`, `claude-sonnet`, `high`\n   130\t   - epic synthesizer: `copilot`, `gpt-5.4`, `xhigh`\n   131\t   Story implementor still falls back to `secondary_harness: \"none\"` because v1 retained implementor continuation is not defined for Copilot.\n   132\t5. If no GPT secondary harness is available, switch all roles to `secondary_harness: \"none\"` and record a degraded-diversity condition in `team-impl-log.md`.\n   133\t\n   134\tThis keeps config authorship with the orchestrator while still satisfying the epic’s requirement that defaults and fallback behavior be resolved deterministically before implementation begins.\n   135\t\n   136\t### `team-impl-log.md`\n   137\t\n   138\t`team-impl-log.md` is the durable orchestration record. It is the primary run-state artifact, not a narrative afterthought. The orchestrator updates it after setup, after each command result, at every story transition, and before epic closeout.\n   139\t\n   140\tRequired sections:\n   141\t\n   142\t| Section | Purpose |\n   143\t|---|---|\n   144\t| Run Overview | spec-pack root, current status, current story, current phase |\n   145\t| Run Configuration | snapshot of the validated `impl-run.config.json` choices |\n   146\t| Verification Gates | story and epic gate commands discovered from project policy |\n   147\t| Story Sequence | full story list and current position |\n   148\t| Receipts | pre-acceptance evidence and dispositions by story |\n   149\t| Cumulative Baselines | test-count expectations and regression checks |\n   150\t| Cleanup / Epic Verification | deferred items, cleanup status, final verification state |\n   151\t| Open Risks / Accepted Risks | explicit unresolved or accepted-risk items |\n   152\t\n   153\tThe log remains markdown because it is both human-facing and agent-facing. The orchestrator needs to read and update it directly. A second support file can be added later if highly structured state becomes necessary, but the v1 feature should keep the required surface small.\n   154\t\n   155\t### State Enum\n   156\t\n   157\tThe log should carry one explicit current-state field using this enum:\n   158\t\n   159\t| State | Meaning |\n   160\t|---|---|\n   161\t| `SETUP` | skill loaded, spec-pack root being resolved, config/log initialization not complete |\n   162\t| `BETWEEN_STORIES` | ready to start the next story |\n   163\t| `STORY_ACTIVE` | implementation / verification / fix routing in progress for one story |\n   164\t| `PRE_EPIC_VERIFY` | all stories accepted, cleanup and epic closeout not yet complete |\n   165\t| `EPIC_VERIFY_ACTIVE` | cleanup, epic verifier batch, or synthesis is in progress |\n   166\t| `COMPLETE` | run complete |\n   167\t| `FAILED` | blocked or failed terminally pending human decision |\n   168\t\n   169\t### `team-impl-log.md` Template\n   170\t\n   171\tThe exact text can evolve, but the heading contract should be fixed.\n   172\t\n   173\t```markdown\n   174\t# Team Implementation Log\n   175\t\n   176\t## Run Overview\n   177\t- State: BETWEEN_STORIES\n   178\t- Spec Pack Root: /abs/path\n   179\t- Current Story: story-03\n   180\t- Current Phase: story-verify\n   181\t\n   182\t## Run Configuration\n   183\t- Primary Harness: claude-code\n   184\t- Story Implementor: codex / gpt-5.4 / high\n   185\t- Quick Fixer: codex / gpt-5.4 / medium\n   186\t- Story Verifier 1: codex / gpt-5.4 / xhigh\n   187\t- Story Verifier 2: none / claude-sonnet / high\n   188\t- Self Review Passes: 3\n   189\t- Degraded Diversity: false\n   190\t\n   191\t## Verification Gates\n   192\t- Story Gate: bun run green-verify\n   193\t- Epic Gate: bun run verify-all\n   194\t- Gate Discovery Source: package.json scripts\n   195\t\n   196\t## Story Sequence\n   197\t- story-00-foundation\n   198\t- story-01-run-setup\n   199\t- story-02-prompt-composition\n   200\t\n   201\t## Current Continuation Handles\n   202\t- Story Implementor:\n   203\t  - Story: story-03\n   204\t  - Provider: codex\n   205\t  - Session ID: abc123\n   206\t\n   207\t## Story Receipts\n   208\t### story-02\n   209\t- Implementor Evidence Ref: artifacts/story-02/implement-result.json\n   210\t- Verifier Evidence Refs:\n   211\t  - artifacts/story-02/verify-primary.json\n   212\t  - artifacts/story-02/verify-secondary.json\n   213\t- Gate Result: pass\n   214\t- Dispositions:\n   215\t  - FIX-1: fixed\n   216\t  - RISK-2: accepted-risk\n   217\t- Open Risks:\n   218\t  - none\n   219\t\n   220\t## Cumulative Baselines\n   221\t- Baseline Before Current Story: 48\n   222\t- Expected After Current Story: 56\n   223\t- Latest Actual Total: 56\n   224\t\n   225\t## Cleanup / Epic Verification\n   226\t- Cleanup Artifact: artifacts/cleanup/cleanup-batch.md\n   227\t- Cleanup Status: not-started\n   228\t- Epic Verification Status: not-started\n   229\t\n   230\t## Open Risks / Accepted Risks\n   231\t- none\n   232\t```\n   233\t\n   234\tThe orchestrator can add prose beneath these headings, but these sections and labels should be present so recovery is deterministic.\n   235\t\n   236\t### Story Receipt Contract\n   237\t\n   238\tEach accepted story should record:\n   239\t\n   240\t- story id and title\n   241\t- implementor result path\n   242\t- verifier result paths\n   243\t- final story gate command + pass/fail outcome\n   244\t- dispositions for each unresolved finding (`fixed`, `accepted-risk`, `defer`)\n   245\t- cumulative test baseline before and after the story\n   246\t- any active continuation handles that remain relevant\n   247\t\n   248\t## Verification Gate Discovery\n   249\t\n   250\tGate discovery must follow a fixed precedence order so the orchestrator does not improvise.\n   251\t\n   252\t1. Explicit CLI flags supplied for the current run\n   253\t2. Explicit entries in `impl-run.config.json` if later added\n   254\t3. package scripts in the repo root\n   255\t4. repository policy docs (`AGENTS.md`, `README`, related process docs)\n   256\t5. CI configuration as supporting evidence\n   257\t6. if still ambiguous: pause with `needs-user-decision`\n   258\t\n   259\tThe playbook should teach the orchestrator to record:\n   260\t\n   261\t- the chosen story gate command\n   262\t- the chosen epic gate command\n   263\t- the source from which each was derived\n   264\t- any ambiguity note that required human confirmation\n   265\t\n   266\t## Flow 1: Skill Load, Startup Orientation, and Basic Setup\n   267\t\n   268\t**Covers:** AC-1.3, AC-1.5, AC-1.6, AC-2.4, AC-6.3\n   269\t\n   270\tThis flow starts when the user explicitly asks Claude Code to load `ls-claude-impl`. The skill’s job at this stage is to orient the orchestrator quickly, resolve the spec-pack root, confirm the minimum required files exist, initialize the orchestrator-owned artifacts, and tell the orchestrator what to read next. The skill should not dump the full methodology immediately. It should get the orchestrator onto the rails with minimal context cost.\n   271\t\n   272\tThe first pass is intentionally shallow. It asks: “Do we have the right folder? Can we initialize durable state? What should the orchestrator read next?” The second pass, driven by the reading journey and CLI preflight, asks the deeper question: “Is the run actually executable?”\n   273\t\n   274\t```mermaid\n   275\tsequenceDiagram\n   276\t    participant User\n   277\t    participant Orchestrator as Claude Code Orchestrator\n   278\t    participant Skill as ls-claude-impl SKILL.md\n   279\t    participant SpecPack as Spec-Pack Root\n   280\t    participant Log as team-impl-log.md\n   281\t\n   282\t    Note over User,Orchestrator: AC-1.3, AC-1.5, AC-1.6\n   283\t    User->>Orchestrator: \"Load ls-claude-impl\"\n   284\t    Orchestrator->>Skill: Load startup instructions\n   285\t    Skill-->>Orchestrator: Resolve spec-pack root + initial checks\n   286\t    Orchestrator->>SpecPack: Confirm epic / tech-design / test-plan / stories presence\n   287\t    Orchestrator->>Log: Create or resume durable run log\n   288\t    Skill-->>Orchestrator: Read claude-impl-reading-journey.md next\n   289\t```\n   290\t\n   291\t### Deliverables Created in This Flow\n   292\t\n   293\t| What | Where | Purpose |\n   294\t|---|---|---|\n   295\t| Skill entrypoint | `src/phases/claude-impl.md` | Startup orientation + next-step dispatch |\n   296\t| Reading-journey reference | `src/references/claude-impl-reading-journey.md` | Controlled next read after setup |\n   297\t| `impl-run.config.json` | spec-pack root | Run configuration owned by orchestrator |\n   298\t| `team-impl-log.md` | spec-pack root | Durable orchestration state and receipts |\n   299\t\n   300\t### TC Mapping for This Flow\n   301\t\n   302\t| TC | Tests | Module | Setup | Assert |\n   303\t|---|---|---|---|---|\n   304\t| TC-1.3a | initializes new run log when absent | `inspect` + skill build tests | fixture spec pack without log | skill/setup path creates `team-impl-log.md` guidance and CLI reports ready for new run |\n   305\t| TC-1.3b | resumes existing run log | `inspect` + fixture tests | fixture spec pack with existing log | setup path reports resume behavior and does not restart from scratch |\n   306\t| TC-1.5a | full story inventory recorded before start | `inspect` contract tests | multiple stories in fixture | story order is returned and intended for log recording |\n   307\t| TC-1.6a | verification gates discovered and recorded | reading-journey/playbook tests | repo fixture with policy docs/scripts | skill guidance and initialization flow require gate capture before implementation |\n   308\t| TC-1.6b | ambiguous gate policy pauses setup | playbook tests | fixture with missing/ambiguous gate commands | skill surface directs user-resolution pause before execution |\n   309\t| TC-2.4a | run configuration recorded | skill + CLI integration tests | valid config + preflight result | orchestrator guidance records validated configuration in log |\n   310\t| TC-6.3a | resume after interruption | process-playbook tests | pre-existing log and config files | skill surface resumes from durable artifacts |\n   311\t| TC-6.3b | resume after context stripping | process-playbook tests | no prior conversation context | guidance relies only on files on disk |\n   312\t\n   313\t## Flow 2: Progressive Reading Journey\n   314\t\n   315\t**Covers:** AC-1.5, AC-3.4\n   316\t\n   317\tOnce the spec-pack root is known and the basic files are present, the orchestrator needs a deliberate reading path. The reading journey exists to prevent both under-reading and over-reading. If the orchestrator reads everything up front, it burns context and weakens later attention. If it reads too little, it makes routing decisions without understanding the feature.\n   318\t\n   319\tThe reading-journey reference should therefore do three things. It should define the read order. It should say what to extract into durable state after each read. And it should explicitly delay lower-value reads until they are needed. The orchestrator should come out of the journey with a stable mental model of the story queue, the tech-design shape, the verification gates, and the current run configuration.\n   320\t\n   321\t```mermaid\n   322\tsequenceDiagram\n   323\t    participant Orchestrator\n   324\t    participant Journey as reading-journey.md\n   325\t    participant Epic\n   326\t    participant TechDesign as tech-design.md + companions\n   327\t    participant TestPlan as test-plan.md\n   328\t    participant Stories as stories/\n   329\t    participant Log as team-impl-log.md\n   330\t\n   331\t    Note over Orchestrator,Log: AC-1.5, AC-3.4\n   332\t    Orchestrator->>Journey: Read ordered reading plan\n   333\t    Journey-->>Orchestrator: Read tech-design index first\n   334\t    Orchestrator->>TechDesign: Read index and note top-tier surfaces\n   335\t    Orchestrator->>TestPlan: Read gate and coverage expectations\n   336\t    Orchestrator->>Epic: Read feature scope and story context\n   337\t    Orchestrator->>Stories: Read all story files in order\n   338\t    Orchestrator->>Log: Record story order, dependencies, and key extracted state\n   339\t```\n   340\t\n   341\t### Reading Journey Contract\n   342\t\n   343\tThe reading-journey reference should tell the orchestrator to extract and record:\n   344\t\n   345\t- story order and dependency notes\n   346\t- story ids using the canonical filename-derived convention\n   347\t- whether the tech design is a 2-doc or 4-doc pack\n   348\t- the presence of prompt insert files\n   349\t- project verification commands\n   350\t- any current blockers or ambiguous prerequisites\n   351\t\n   352\tIt should not ask the orchestrator to load provider-specific invocation details at this stage. Those belong in the CLI operations and prompt-system references and are only needed once initialization has succeeded.\n   353\t\n   354\t### TC Mapping for This Flow\n   355\t\n   356\t| TC | Tests | Module | Setup | Assert |\n   357\t|---|---|---|---|---|\n   358\t| TC-1.5a | reading journey reads all stories before start | reference doc tests | fixture with multiple stories | guide requires full story inventory before Story 0/1 |\n   359\t| TC-3.4a | implementor reading journey defined | prompt-system + reading-journey tests | skill reference files | implementor journey includes story, tech-design set, bounded reflection behavior |\n   360\t| TC-3.4b | verifier reading journey defined | prompt-system + reading-journey tests | skill reference files | verifier journey includes evidence-focused read instructions |\n   361\t| TC-3.4c | quick-fix handoff narrows context | prompt-system tests | quick-fix section | quick fixer receives bounded context rather than full reread |\n   362\t\n   363\t## Flow 3: Orchestrator State Ownership, Progression, and Recovery\n   364\t\n   365\t**Covers:** AC-6.1, AC-6.2, AC-6.3, AC-7.1, AC-7.2, AC-7.3\n   366\t\n   367\tThe orchestrator is responsible for durable state, not just command dispatch. That means the skill surface must explicitly teach when to write receipts, when to update baselines, when to materialize cleanup items, and how to resume after interruption. If that behavior is not written into the skill clearly, the orchestrator will improvise, and the run will drift.\n   368\t\n   369\tThis feature’s recovery model is intentionally boring. The orchestrator resumes from disk, not from memory. It reads `team-impl-log.md`, `impl-run.config.json`, and any command result artifacts that the CLI previously returned. That is the full contract. No hidden runtime state in the CLI should be required to understand what story is active, what verification round is current, or what cleanup remains.\n   370\t\n   371\t```mermaid\n   372\tsequenceDiagram\n   373\t    participant Orchestrator\n   374\t    participant CLI\n   375\t    participant Result as CLI Result JSON\n   376\t    participant Log as team-impl-log.md\n   377\t    participant Cleanup as Cleanup Artifact\n   378\t\n   379\t    Note over Orchestrator,Cleanup: AC-6.1 to AC-7.3\n   380\t    Orchestrator->>CLI: Run bounded operation\n   381\t    CLI-->>Result: Structured outcome + evidence refs\n   382\t    Orchestrator->>Log: Record receipt, gate result, dispositions, baselines\n   383\t    Orchestrator->>Cleanup: Materialize deferred/accepted-risk items when needed\n   384\t    Orchestrator->>Log: Record cleanup review and epic-transition state\n   385\t```\n   386\t\n   387\t### Durable Artifact Rules\n   388\t\n   389\t| Artifact | Owner | Required Use |\n   390\t|---|---|---|\n   391\t| `impl-run.config.json` | orchestrator | declare run shape before preflight |\n   392\t| `team-impl-log.md` | orchestrator | record state transitions, receipts, baselines, and cleanup |\n   393\t| command result JSON | CLI | return explicit outcome and evidence for the orchestrator to persist |\n   394\t| cleanup artifact | orchestrator | materialize deferred/accepted-risk items before epic verification |\n   395\t\n   396\t### TC Mapping for This Flow\n   397\t\n   398\t| TC | Tests | Module | Setup | Assert |\n   399\t|---|---|---|---|---|\n   400\t| TC-6.1a | pre-acceptance receipt required | process-playbook tests | simulated accepted story result | log contract requires evidence, gate results, dispositions, risks |\n   401\t| TC-6.2a | cumulative progression uses durable artifacts | process-playbook tests | prior accepted story + log state | next-story preparation uses codebase/log/story order, not memory |\n   402\t| TC-6.2b | test-count regression blocks progression | process-playbook tests | lower-than-expected cumulative total | orchestrator guidance blocks acceptance until resolved |\n   403\t| TC-6.3a | resume after interruption from files | recovery tests | interrupted run fixture | skill resumes from log + config + result files |\n   404\t| TC-6.3b | resume without conversation context | recovery tests | fresh session with same files | progression remains possible from disk only |\n   405\t| TC-7.1a | cleanup list materialized before epic verification | cleanup tests | completed stories with deferred items | cleanup artifact is required before epic verification |\n   406\t| TC-7.2a | human review of cleanup batch required | process-playbook tests | cleanup artifact present | skill requires review before dispatch |\n   407\t| TC-7.3a | cleanup verified before epic verification | cleanup tests | approved cleanup fixes | process requires cleanup verification before epic verifier launch |\n   408\t\n   409\t## Flow 4: Process Playbook and Command Routing\n   410\t\n   411\t**Covers:** AC-4.1 to AC-5.5, AC-8.1 to AC-8.4\n   412\t\n   413\tThe orchestrator does not need to know internal provider-invocation mechanics, but it does need a clear operational map: which command corresponds to which process stage, what each one expects, and what to do after it returns. That is the purpose of the process playbook and CLI-operations references.\n   414\t\n   415\tThe mapping should feel obvious:\n   416\t\n   417\t- use `inspect` for structural discovery\n   418\t- use `preflight` when the reading journey is complete and the run config exists\n   419\t- use `story-implement` to start a story\n   420\t- use `story-continue` only when the existing implementation session should continue\n   421\t- use `story-verify` for fresh verifier batches\n   422\t- use `quick-fix` for bounded corrections\n   423\t- use `epic-cleanup`, `epic-verify`, and `epic-synthesize` at closeout\n   424\t\n   425\tThe playbook should also teach the orchestrator what *not* to do. It should not rewrite code directly, skip final gates, or silently downgrade missing provider lanes. If the CLI returns `needs-user-decision` or `blocked`, the playbook should say exactly what kind of pause is required and what evidence to review.\n   426\t\n   427\t### Process Map\n   428\t\n   429\t```mermaid\n   430\tflowchart TD\n   431\t    A[Load skill] --> B[Resolve spec-pack root]\n   432\t    B --> C[Read reading journey]\n   433\t    C --> D[Write impl-run.config.json]\n   434\t    D --> E[Run ls-impl-cli preflight]\n   435\t    E -->|ready| F[story-implement]\n   436\t    F --> G[story-verify]\n   437\t    G -->|small bounded fix| H[quick-fix]\n   438\t    G -->|same-session follow-up| I[story-continue]\n   439\t    G -->|pass| J[record receipt + next story]\n   440\t    J -->|more stories| F\n   441\t    J -->|all stories done| K[epic-cleanup]\n   442\t    K --> L[epic-verify]\n   443\t    L --> M[epic-synthesize]\n   444\t    M --> N[final epic gate + closeout]\n   445\t```\n   446\t\n   447\t### Process Playbook Responsibilities\n   448\t\n   449\t| Stage | Orchestrator Responsibility |\n   450\t|---|---|\n   451\t| Setup | locate spec pack, initialize config/log, read first references |\n   452\t| Preflight | validate readiness before any provider-backed work starts |\n   453\t| Story implementation | choose the story, run implementation, preserve continuation handles |\n   454\t| Story verification | launch fresh verification, interpret findings, route fixes |\n   455\t| Story progression | record receipts, update cumulative baselines, move to next story |\n   456\t| Cleanup / epic verification | materialize deferred items, review cleanup, run epic verifier and synthesizer workflows |\n   457\t\n   458\t### TC Mapping for This Flow\n   459\t\n   460\t| TC | Tests | Module | Setup | Assert |\n   461\t|---|---|---|---|---|\n   462\t| TC-4.1a | process playbook routes story start to `story-implement` | CLI operation guide tests | story ready to start | skill maps stage to correct command |\n   463\t| TC-5.5a | final story gate remains orchestrator-owned | process-playbook tests | verification appears complete | playbook requires orchestrator-run acceptance gate |\n   464\t| TC-8.1a | multi-story epic requires epic-level verification | process-playbook tests | all stories accepted | playbook routes to epic verification instead of closeout |\n   465\t| TC-8.1b | no skip path for epic verification | playbook tests | epic ready to close | documentation exposes no skip route |\n   466\t| TC-8.2a | synthesis required every run | playbook tests | epic verifier results available | playbook requires synthesis before closeout |\n   467\t| TC-8.4a | final epic gate remains orchestrator-owned | playbook tests | synthesis complete | skill requires final gate by orchestrator |\n   468\t\n   469\t## Document Contracts for This Domain\n   470\t\n   471\t### `src/phases/claude-impl.md`\n   472\t\n   473\tThe skill entrypoint should contain only:\n   474\t\n   475\t- short startup orientation\n   476\t- minimum artifact expectations\n   477\t- initialization steps\n   478\t- state ownership rules\n   479\t- explicit “read this next” instructions\n   480\t\n   481\tIt should not inline:\n   482\t\n   483\t- every CLI command detail\n   484\t- every stage rule\n   485\t- every prompt-system detail\n   486\t\n   487\tThose belong in references so the orchestrator loads them only when the process reaches the relevant depth.\n   488\t\n   489\t### `src/references/claude-impl-process-playbook.md`\n   490\t\n   491\tThe process playbook should be the orchestrator’s main reference after setup. It should include:\n   492\t\n   493\t- stage map\n   494\t- command routing\n   495\t- receipt requirements\n   496\t- progression rules\n   497\t- recovery rules\n   498\t- cleanup and closeout rules\n   499\t\n   500\t### `src/references/claude-impl-cli-operations.md`\n   501\t\n   502\tThis file should present each CLI command from the orchestrator’s perspective:\n   503\t\n   504\t- when to use it\n   505\t- what files or inputs it expects\n   506\t- what the outcome states mean\n   507\t- what the orchestrator should do next\n   508\t\n   509\tThe emphasis should be on legibility, not implementation detail.\n   510\t\n   511\t## Related Sections\n   512\t\n   513\t- Cross-cutting architecture and dependency choices: `tech-design.md`\n   514\t- CLI/runtime command model, prompt assets, and provider adapters: `tech-design-cli-runtime.md`\n   515\t- TC-to-test coverage and fixture strategy: `test-plan.md`\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
docs/spec-build/epics/01-claude-impl-cli-skill/artifacts/story-02/006-verifier-codex53-high.jsonl:14:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_6","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc 'nl -ba docs/spec-build/epics/01-claude-impl-cli-skill/tech-design-cli-runtime.md'","aggregated_output":"     1\t# Technical Design Companion: CLI Runtime Surface\n     2\t\n     3\t## Purpose\n     4\t\n     5\tThis companion covers the `ls-impl-cli` runtime that executes bounded operations on behalf of the `ls-claude-impl` orchestrator. The CLI is not the methodology. It is the execution surface that makes the methodology practical. It validates readiness, assembles provider-facing prompts, calls secondary harnesses when configured, captures structured outputs, and returns disciplined result contracts to the orchestrator.\n     6\t\n     7\tThe design goal is an agent-first CLI. Every command should correspond to a clear process action that the orchestrator already understands from the skill docs. The CLI should hide internal complexity but not produce mystery. It should accept explicit inputs, return explicit results, and avoid holding orchestration lifecycle in hidden runtime state between calls.\n     8\t\n     9\t## Runtime Surface Overview\n    10\t\n    11\tThe runtime has five responsibilities:\n    12\t\n    13\t1. Resolve and validate the spec-pack surface.\n    14\t2. Load and validate the orchestrator-owned run config.\n    15\t3. Assemble prompts from embedded base prompts, snippets, runtime values, and public inserts.\n    16\t4. Execute one bounded operation through the appropriate primary/secondary harness combination.\n    17\t5. Return structured results that the orchestrator can persist and act on.\n    18\t\n    19\tThe runtime does **not** own:\n    20\t\n    21\t- story progression\n    22\t- acceptance decisions\n    23\t- cumulative baselines across the whole run\n    24\t- cleanup review decisions\n    25\t- epic closeout decisions\n    26\t\n    27\tThose remain with the orchestrator.\n    28\t\n    29\t## Shared Result Envelope\n    30\t\n    31\tEvery command should return the same outer JSON envelope so the orchestrator can ingest results without command-specific parsing rules.\n    32\t\n    33\t```typescript\n    34\texport interface CliError {\n    35\t  code: string;\n    36\t  message: string;\n    37\t  detail?: string;\n    38\t}\n    39\t\n    40\texport interface CliArtifactRef {\n    41\t  kind: string;\n    42\t  path: string;\n    43\t}\n    44\t\n    45\texport interface CliResultEnvelope<T> {\n    46\t  command: string;\n    47\t  version: 1;\n    48\t  status: \"ok\" | \"needs-user-decision\" | \"blocked\" | \"error\";\n    49\t  outcome: string;\n    50\t  result?: T;\n    51\t  errors: CliError[];\n    52\t  warnings: string[];\n    53\t  artifacts: CliArtifactRef[];\n    54\t  startedAt: string;\n    55\t  finishedAt: string;\n    56\t}\n    57\t```\n    58\t\n    59\tThe JSON envelope is the source of truth. Human-readable `stderr` is allowed for progress or debugging, but the orchestrator should route decisions from the envelope rather than from ad hoc terminal text.\n    60\t\n    61\tSuggested stable error codes:\n    62\t\n    63\t- `INVALID_SPEC_PACK`\n    64\t- `INVALID_RUN_CONFIG`\n    65\t- `VERIFICATION_GATE_UNRESOLVED`\n    66\t- `PROVIDER_UNAVAILABLE`\n    67\t- `PROVIDER_OUTPUT_INVALID`\n    68\t- `CONTINUATION_HANDLE_INVALID`\n    69\t- `PROMPT_ASSET_MISSING`\n    70\t- `PROMPT_INSERT_INVALID`\n    71\t\n    72\t## Public CLI Contract\n    73\t\n    74\tThe public CLI contract consists of:\n    75\t\n    76\t1. command signatures in `Command Signatures`\n    77\t2. shared JSON envelope in `Shared Result Envelope`\n    78\t3. stdout/stderr/artifact behavior in `Public CLI IO Contract`\n    79\t4. exit-code and routing behavior in `Exit Codes` and `Result Routing Matrix`\n    80\t5. provider execution assumptions in `Provider Invocation Contracts`\n    81\t\n    82\tAn implementation that matches those five surfaces should be interchangeable at the orchestrator boundary even if internal module structure changes later.\n    83\t\n    84\t## Command Surface\n    85\t\n    86\tThe public CLI surface should be small and stable.\n    87\t\n    88\t| Command | Purpose | Required Inputs | Primary Outputs | Outcome States |\n    89\t|---|---|---|---|---|\n    90\t| `inspect` | Resolve spec-pack layout and story inventory | spec-pack root | spec-pack summary, story order, insert presence | `ready`, `needs-user-decision`, `blocked` |\n    91\t| `preflight` | Validate run config, provider availability, prompt assets, and run readiness | spec-pack root, `impl-run.config.json` | readiness report, validated config, provider matrix | `ready`, `needs-user-decision`, `blocked` |\n    92\t| `story-implement` | Start implementation for one story | story id, spec-pack root, run config | implementor result, continuation handle | `ready-for-verification`, `needs-followup-fix`, `needs-human-ruling`, `blocked` |\n    93\t| `story-continue` | Continue a retained implementor session for the current story | continuation handle, follow-up request | continued implementor result | `ready-for-verification`, `needs-followup-fix`, `needs-human-ruling`, `blocked` |\n    94\t| `story-verify` | Run a fresh verifier batch for one story | story id, run config, verification context | verifier batch result array | `pass`, `revise`, `block` |\n    95\t| `quick-fix` | Apply a narrow, bounded fix request | fix request, affected scope, role config | quick-fix result | `ready-for-verification`, `needs-more-routing`, `blocked` |\n    96\t| `epic-cleanup` | Apply cleanup-only corrections before epic verification | cleanup batch, selected fix workflow | cleanup result | `cleaned`, `needs-more-cleanup`, `blocked` |\n    97\t| `epic-verify` | Run fresh epic-level verification | spec-pack root, run config | epic verifier result array | `pass`, `revise`, `block` |\n    98\t| `epic-synthesize` | Verify and consolidate epic-level findings | epic verifier results, run config | synthesis result | `ready-for-closeout`, `needs-fixes`, `needs-more-verification`, `blocked` |\n    99\t\n   100\tThe CLI with no arguments should print quick-start help only. The full process explanation stays in the skill docs.\n   101\t\n   102\t### Command Signatures\n   103\t\n   104\tThe public signatures should stay stable even if internal implementation changes.\n   105\t\n   106\t| Command | Signature |\n   107\t|---|---|\n   108\t| `inspect` | `ls-impl-cli inspect --spec-pack-root <path> [--json]` |\n   109\t| `preflight` | `ls-impl-cli preflight --spec-pack-root <path> [--config <path>] [--story-gate <cmd>] [--epic-gate <cmd>] [--json]` |\n   110\t| `story-implement` | `ls-impl-cli story-implement --spec-pack-root <path> --story-id <id> [--config <path>] [--json]` |\n   111\t| `story-continue` | `ls-impl-cli story-continue --spec-pack-root <path> --story-id <id> --provider <provider> --session-id <id> [--config <path>] [--followup-file <path> | --followup-text <text>] [--json]` |\n   112\t| `story-verify` | `ls-impl-cli story-verify --spec-pack-root <path> --story-id <id> [--config <path>] [--json]` |\n   113\t| `quick-fix` | `ls-impl-cli quick-fix --spec-pack-root <path> --story-id <id> --request-file <path> [--scope-file <path>] [--config <path>] [--json]` |\n   114\t| `epic-cleanup` | `ls-impl-cli epic-cleanup --spec-pack-root <path> --cleanup-batch <path> [--config <path>] [--json]` |\n   115\t| `epic-verify` | `ls-impl-cli epic-verify --spec-pack-root <path> [--config <path>] [--json]` |\n   116\t| `epic-synthesize` | `ls-impl-cli epic-synthesize --spec-pack-root <path> --verifier-report <path> --verifier-report <path> [--config <path>] [--json]` |\n   117\t\n   118\tAll commands should support `--json` for machine-readable output. Human-readable summaries may exist for manual use, but the orchestrator contract should be the JSON payload.\n   119\t\n   120\t### Public CLI IO Contract\n   121\t\n   122\tThe CLI should behave consistently across commands.\n   123\t\n   124\t| Surface | Rule |\n   125\t|---|---|\n   126\t| stdout with `--json` | emit exactly one `CliResultEnvelope<T>` JSON object |\n   127\t| stdout without `--json` | emit a concise human-readable summary only |\n   128\t| stderr | reserved for progress/debugging; never required for orchestration routing |\n   129\t| persisted artifacts | write the same JSON envelope that was emitted on stdout |\n   130\t| default config path | `<spec-pack-root>/impl-run.config.json` unless `--config` overrides it |\n   131\t| default artifact root | `<spec-pack-root>/artifacts/` |\n   132\t\n   133\t### Command Examples\n   134\t\n   135\t```bash\n   136\tnode bin/ls-impl-cli.cjs inspect --spec-pack-root /abs/spec-pack --json\n   137\tnode bin/ls-impl-cli.cjs preflight --spec-pack-root /abs/spec-pack --json\n   138\tnode bin/ls-impl-cli.cjs story-implement --spec-pack-root /abs/spec-pack --story-id story-03 --json\n   139\tnode bin/ls-impl-cli.cjs story-verify --spec-pack-root /abs/spec-pack --story-id story-03 --json\n   140\tnode bin/ls-impl-cli.cjs epic-cleanup --spec-pack-root /abs/spec-pack --cleanup-batch /abs/spec-pack/artifacts/cleanup/cleanup-batch.md --json\n   141\t```\n   142\t\n   143\tFor commands whose inner payload also contains an `outcome`, the envelope `outcome` is the routing source of truth and must match the inner payload exactly. Schema validation should reject mismatches.\n   144\t\n   145\t### Exit Codes\n   146\t\n   147\tExit codes are a coarse routing signal. The JSON payload remains the source of truth.\n   148\t\n   149\t| Exit Code | Meaning |\n   150\t|---|---|\n   151\t| `0` | Command completed and produced a non-blocking outcome (`ready`, `ready-for-verification`, `pass`, `cleaned`, `ready-for-closeout`) |\n   152\t| `2` | Command completed but requires follow-up (`needs-user-decision`, `needs-followup-fix`, `needs-human-ruling`, `revise`, `needs-more-routing`, `needs-more-cleanup`, `needs-fixes`, `needs-more-verification`) |\n   153\t| `3` | Command is blocked by environment, spec-pack, config, or provider readiness issue |\n   154\t| `1` | Invalid invocation, schema parse failure, or uncaught runtime error |\n   155\t\n   156\t### Result Routing Matrix\n   157\t\n   158\tThis matrix is authoritative for handler implementation and tests.\n   159\t\n   160\t| Command Outcome | Envelope Status | Exit Code | Orchestrator Action |\n   161\t|---|---|---:|---|\n   162\t| `ready` | `ok` | 0 | proceed to next setup step |\n   163\t| `ready-for-verification` | `ok` | 0 | run verification or next bounded step |\n   164\t| `pass` | `ok` | 0 | evaluate for acceptance or next stage |\n   165\t| `cleaned` | `ok` | 0 | move into epic verification |\n   166\t| `ready-for-closeout` | `ok` | 0 | run final orchestrator-owned gate |\n   167\t| `needs-user-decision` | `needs-user-decision` | 2 | pause and request human clarification |\n   168\t| `needs-followup-fix` | `ok` | 2 | route same-session follow-up or fix path |\n   169\t| `needs-human-ruling` | `needs-user-decision` | 2 | pause for orchestrator/human ruling |\n   170\t| `revise` | `ok` | 2 | route fix and rerun verification |\n   171\t| `needs-more-routing` | `ok` | 2 | select another bounded correction path |\n   172\t| `needs-more-cleanup` | `ok` | 2 | continue cleanup cycle |\n   173\t| `needs-fixes` | `ok` | 2 | route fixes before closeout |\n   174\t| `needs-more-verification` | `ok` | 2 | launch additional verification/synthesis |\n   175\t| `blocked` | `blocked` | 3 | stop and inspect blocking error details |\n   176\t| parse/schema/runtime failure | `error` | 1 | treat as CLI failure rather than feature-state outcome |\n   177\t\n   178\t## Runtime Module Layout\n   179\t\n   180\t```text\n   181\tprocesses/impl-cli/\n   182\t├── cli.ts\n   183\t├── commands/\n   184\t│   ├── inspect.ts\n   185\t│   ├── preflight.ts\n   186\t│   ├── story-implement.ts\n   187\t│   ├── story-continue.ts\n   188\t│   ├── story-verify.ts\n   189\t│   ├── quick-fix.ts\n   190\t│   ├── epic-cleanup.ts\n   191\t│   ├── epic-verify.ts\n   192\t│   └── epic-synthesize.ts\n   193\t├── core/\n   194\t│   ├── config-schema.ts\n   195\t│   ├── spec-pack.ts\n   196\t│   ├── story-order.ts\n   197\t│   ├── prompt-assets.ts\n   198\t│   ├── prompt-assembly.ts\n   199\t│   ├── provider-checks.ts\n   200\t│   ├── provider-adapters/\n   201\t│   │   ├── claude-code.ts\n   202\t│   │   ├── codex.ts\n   203\t│   │   └── copilot.ts\n   204\t│   ├── result-contracts.ts\n   205\t│   ├── status-codes.ts\n   206\t│   ├── command-context.ts\n   207\t│   └── embedded-assets.generated.ts\n   208\t└── prompts/\n   209\t    ├── base/\n   210\t    └── snippets/\n   211\t```\n   212\t\n   213\tThe command modules stay thin. They parse command-specific flags with `citty`, call into core services, and print structured JSON. All real behavior lives in `core/`.\n   214\t\n   215\t### Core Service Interfaces\n   216\t\n   217\tThe command handlers should depend on a small set of explicit core interfaces.\n   218\t\n   219\t```typescript\n   220\texport interface SpecPackResolver {\n   221\t  resolve(specPackRoot: string): Promise<InspectResult>;\n   222\t}\n   223\t\n   224\texport interface VerificationGateResolver {\n   225\t  resolve(input: {\n   226\t    specPackRoot: string;\n   227\t    explicitStoryGate?: string;\n   228\t    explicitEpicGate?: string;\n   229\t  }): Promise<VerificationGateConfig | CliError>;\n   230\t}\n   231\t\n   232\texport interface ArtifactWriter {\n   233\t  write<T>(params: {\n   234\t    specPackRoot: string;\n   235\t    command: string;\n   236\t    storyId?: string;\n   237\t    payload: CliResultEnvelope<T>;\n   238\t  }): Promise<CliArtifactRef>;\n   239\t}\n   240\t\n   241\texport interface ProviderResultParser<T> {\n   242\t  parse(raw: ProviderExecutionResult): Promise<T | CliError>;\n   243\t}\n   244\t```\n   245\t\n   246\tThese interfaces are deliberately smaller than the whole runtime. They pin the boundaries that are most likely to drift during implementation.\n   247\t\n   248\t## Run Configuration Contract\n   249\t\n   250\tThe CLI consumes an explicit orchestrator-owned JSON file. The contract is intentionally lean.\n   251\t\n   252\t```typescript\n   253\texport type PrimaryHarness = \"claude-code\";\n   254\texport type SecondaryHarness = \"codex\" | \"copilot\" | \"none\";\n   255\texport type ReasoningEffort = \"low\" | \"medium\" | \"high\" | \"xhigh\";\n   256\t\n   257\texport interface RoleAssignment {\n   258\t  secondary_harness: SecondaryHarness;\n   259\t  model: string;\n   260\t  reasoning_effort: ReasoningEffort;\n   261\t}\n   262\t\n   263\texport interface EpicVerifierAssignment extends RoleAssignment {\n   264\t  label: string;\n   265\t}\n   266\t\n   267\texport interface ImplRunConfig {\n   268\t  version: 1;\n   269\t  primary_harness: PrimaryHarness;\n   270\t  story_implementor: RoleAssignment;\n   271\t  quick_fixer: RoleAssignment;\n   272\t  story_verifier_1: RoleAssignment;\n   273\t  story_verifier_2: RoleAssignment;\n   274\t  self_review: {\n   275\t    passes: number;\n   276\t  };\n   277\t  epic_verifiers: EpicVerifierAssignment[];\n   278\t  epic_synthesizer: RoleAssignment;\n   279\t}\n   280\t```\n   281\t\n   282\tCanonical display labels:\n   283\t\n   284\t| Stored Value | Display Label |\n   285\t|---|---|\n   286\t| `low` | low |\n   287\t| `medium` | medium |\n   288\t| `high` | high |\n   289\t| `xhigh` | extra high |\n   290\t\n   291\t`c12` should be used in explicit-file mode for this feature. The CLI should load either the path passed via `--config` or the default `impl-run.config.json` in the spec-pack root. It should not enable multi-source discovery from home config, `package.json`, or environment overlays in v1. The orchestrator benefits from one obvious config file and one obvious precedence model.\n   292\t\n   293\t### Validation Rules\n   294\t\n   295\t| Rule | Reason |\n   296\t|---|---|\n   297\t| `primary_harness` must be `claude-code` in v1 | v1 always runs inside Claude Code |\n   298\t| `secondary_harness` must be `codex`, `copilot`, or `none` | keep the contract explicit and narrow |\n   299\t| `none` means “use primary harness” | avoids extra indirection or derived config fields |\n   300\t| `story_implementor.secondary_harness !== \"copilot\"` in v1 | Copilot continuation is not supported for the retained implementor role |\n   301\t| `self_review.passes` must be `>= 1` and `<= 5` | prevents nonsensical or runaway values |\n   302\t| `epic_verifiers.length >= 1` | epic verification is mandatory |\n   303\t| duplicate epic verifier labels are invalid | makes downstream reporting legible |\n   304\t\n   305\t### Provider Availability Matrix\n   306\t\n   307\tThe CLI must validate not just schema shape, but whether the local machine can honor the requested harness assignments.\n   308\t\n   309\t```typescript\n   310\texport interface HarnessAvailability {\n   311\t  harness: SecondaryHarness | PrimaryHarness;\n   312\t  available: boolean;\n   313\t  version?: string;\n   314\t  authStatus?: \"authenticated\" | \"unknown\" | \"missing\";\n   315\t  notes: string[];\n   316\t}\n   317\t\n   318\texport interface ProviderMatrix {\n   319\t  primary: HarnessAvailability;\n   320\t  secondary: HarnessAvailability[];\n   321\t}\n   322\t```\n   323\t\n   324\tValidation behavior:\n   325\t\n   326\t- Always validate `claude-code` availability because it is the primary harness.\n   327\t- Validate `codex` only if at least one role requests `secondary_harness: \"codex\"`.\n   328\t- Validate `copilot` only if at least one role requests `secondary_harness: \"copilot\"`.\n   329\t- Skip provider-CLI checks for roles using `secondary_harness: \"none\"`.\n   330\t\n   331\t## Prompt Asset System\n   332\t\n   333\tPrompt assets are runtime architecture, not incidental text blobs. They should live in source form for maintainability, then be embedded into a generated TS module so the packaged runtime can ship as a single JS file.\n   334\t\n   335\t### Prompt Asset Layout\n   336\t\n   337\t```text\n   338\tprocesses/impl-cli/prompts/\n   339\t├── base/\n   340\t│   ├── story-implementor.md\n   341\t│   ├── story-verifier.md\n   342\t│   ├── quick-fixer.md\n   343\t│   ├── epic-verifier.md\n   344\t│   └── epic-synthesizer.md\n   345\t└── snippets/\n   346\t    ├── reading-journey.md\n   347\t    ├── report-contract.md\n   348\t    ├── gate-instructions.md\n   349\t    ├── mock-audit.md\n   350\t    ├── self-review-pass-1.md\n   351\t    ├── self-review-pass-2.md\n   352\t    └── self-review-pass-3.md\n   353\t```\n   354\t\n   355\t### Prompt Asset Content Contracts\n   356\t\n   357\tEach prompt asset should have a required internal contract, not just a filename.\n   358\t\n   359\t| Asset | Required Content |\n   360\t|---|---|\n   361\t| `story-implementor.md` | role stance, artifact reading order, implementation scope, self-review rules, JSON result contract |\n   362\t| `story-verifier.md` | evidence rules, severity enum, AC/TC coverage expectations, mock/shim audit rules, routing guidance |\n   363\t| `quick-fixer.md` | narrow-scope rule, forbidden full-story reimplementation rule, result contract |\n   364\t| `epic-verifier.md` | cross-story checks, architecture consistency, production-path mock/shim audit, verifier result contract |\n   365\t| `epic-synthesizer.md` | independent verification duty, confirmed vs disputed issue categories, synthesis result contract |\n   366\t| shared snippets | explicit placeholders, allowed runtime variables, and insertion order guarantees |\n   367\t\n   368\t### Prompt Assembly Pipeline\n   369\t\n   370\tPrompt assembly should be deterministic.\n   371\t\n   372\t1. Choose the role.\n   373\t2. Load that role’s base prompt.\n   374\t3. Append required reusable snippets for the operation.\n   375\t4. Append the relevant public insert file if present.\n   376\t5. Interpolate runtime values such as story path, tech-design shape, gate commands, and continuation handles.\n   377\t6. Emit one provider-ready prompt string and a compact assembly manifest for debugging.\n   378\t\n   379\t```typescript\n   380\texport interface PromptAssemblyInput {\n   381\t  role:\n   382\t    | \"story_implementor\"\n   383\t    | \"story_verifier\"\n   384\t    | \"quick_fixer\"\n   385\t    | \"epic_verifier\"\n   386\t    | \"epic_synthesizer\";\n   387\t  storyId?: string;\n   388\t  storyPath?: string;\n   389\t  epicPath: string;\n   390\t  techDesignPath: string;\n   391\t  techDesignCompanionPaths: string[];\n   392\t  testPlanPath: string;\n   393\t  implementationPromptInsertPath?: string;\n   394\t  verifierPromptInsertPath?: string;\n   395\t  gateCommands: {\n   396\t    story?: string;\n   397\t    epic?: string;\n   398\t  };\n   399\t  selfReviewPass?: number;\n   400\t  followupRequest?: string;\n   401\t}\n   402\t\n   403\texport interface PromptAssemblyResult {\n   404\t  prompt: string;\n   405\t  basePromptId: string;\n   406\t  snippetIds: string[];\n   407\t  publicInsertIds: string[];\n   408\t}\n   409\t```\n   410\t\n   411\tThe orchestrator does not need to see every runtime prompt asset, but the prompt-system reference should explain the mental model: base prompt plus snippets plus public insert plus runtime values.\n   412\t\n   413\tFor implementation, prompt assembly inputs should be modeled as discriminated unions by operation rather than as one giant loose object. The shared fields above describe the common shape; command-specific inputs should narrow them for:\n   414\t\n   415\t- story implement / continue\n   416\t- story verify\n   417\t- quick fix\n   418\t- epic cleanup\n   419\t- epic verify\n   420\t- epic synthesize\n   421\t\n   422\t## Flow 1: Inspect and Preflight\n   423\t\n   424\t**Covers:** AC-1.1, AC-1.2, AC-1.4, AC-1.5, AC-1.6, AC-2.1 to AC-2.4\n   425\t\n   426\tThis is the CLI’s setup boundary. `inspect` answers the structural question: is the spec pack arranged in a way the runtime understands? `preflight` answers the executable question: can this specific machine run the requested harness/model configuration with the required prompt assets and files in place?\n   427\t\n   428\tThis split matters. The orchestrator should be able to inspect a spec pack before it has chosen or authored a run config, but it should not launch any provider-backed work until preflight has passed.\n   429\t\n   430\t```mermaid\n   431\tsequenceDiagram\n   432\t    participant Orchestrator\n   433\t    participant CLI\n   434\t    participant SpecPack\n   435\t    participant Config as impl-run.config.json\n   436\t    participant Providers as Provider CLIs\n   437\t\n   438\t    Note over Orchestrator,Providers: AC-1.1 to AC-2.4\n   439\t    Orchestrator->>CLI: inspect --spec-pack-root <path>\n   440\t    CLI->>SpecPack: Resolve required files, story order, insert presence\n   441\t    CLI-->>Orchestrator: Spec-pack summary\n   442\t    Orchestrator->>CLI: preflight --spec-pack-root <path>\n   443\t    CLI->>Config: Parse and validate run config\n   444\t    CLI->>Providers: Validate requested harness availability\n   445\t    CLI-->>Orchestrator: Readiness report + validated provider matrix\n   446\t```\n   447\t\n   448\t### Inspect Result Contract\n   449\t\n   450\t```typescript\n   451\texport interface InspectResult {\n   452\t  status: \"ready\" | \"needs-user-decision\" | \"blocked\";\n   453\t  specPackRoot: string;\n   454\t  techDesignShape: \"two-file\" | \"four-file\";\n   455\t  artifacts: {\n   456\t    epicPath: string;\n   457\t    techDesignPath: string;\n   458\t    techDesignCompanionPaths: string[];\n   459\t    testPlanPath: string;\n   460\t    storiesDir: string;\n   461\t  };\n   462\t  stories: Array<{ id: string; title: string; path: string; order: number }>;\n   463\t  inserts: {\n   464\t    customStoryImplPromptInsert: \"present\" | \"absent\";\n   465\t    customStoryVerifierPromptInsert: \"present\" | \"absent\";\n   466\t  };\n   467\t  blockers: string[];\n   468\t  notes: string[];\n   469\t}\n   470\t```\n   471\t\n   472\t### Preflight Result Contract\n   473\t\n   474\t```typescript\n   475\texport interface PreflightResult {\n   476\t  status: \"ready\" | \"needs-user-decision\" | \"blocked\";\n   477\t  validatedConfig: ImplRunConfig;\n   478\t  providerMatrix: ProviderMatrix;\n   479\t  verificationGates?: {\n   480\t    storyGate: string;\n   481\t    epicGate: string;\n   482\t    source: string;\n   483\t  };\n   484\t  configValidationNotes: string[];\n   485\t  promptAssets: {\n   486\t    basePromptsReady: boolean;\n   487\t    snippetsReady: boolean;\n   488\t    notes: string[];\n   489\t  };\n   490\t  blockers: string[];\n   491\t  notes: string[];\n   492\t}\n   493\t```\n   494\t\n   495\t`preflight` validates the authored config and reports the consequences of that config. It does **not** generate or rewrite the default matrix in v1. Default selection is part of the skill/process contract and is owned by the orchestrator before preflight runs.\n   496\t\n   497\t### Story Ordering and Story ID Rules\n   498\t\n   499\tSpec-pack resolution must not invent story order heuristically.\n   500\t\n   501\t1. Read markdown files from `stories/`, excluding `coverage.md`.\n   502\t2. Derive `storyId` from the filename without extension.\n   503\t3. Order stories by the leading numeric prefix in the filename when present.\n   504\t4. If two files have the same numeric prefix, fall back to lexical filename order.\n   505\t5. If no numeric prefixes exist anywhere, use lexical filename order and emit a warning in `inspect`.\n   506\t6. If the naming pattern is mixed and produces ambiguous ordering, return `needs-user-decision`.\n   507\t\n   508\tThis keeps story order reproducible and makes the ordering rules explicit for both runtime code and tests.\n   509\t\n   510\tExamples:\n   511\t\n   512\t- `00-foundation.md`, `01-run-setup.md`, `02-prompts.md` -> valid, ordered numerically\n   513\t- `01-a.md`, `01-b.md` -> valid, ordered lexically within the same prefix\n   514\t- `01-a.md`, `b.md`, `02-c.md` -> ambiguous mixed pattern, return `needs-user-decision`\n   515\t\n   516\t### Tech Design Companion Discovery Rules\n   517\t\n   518\tThe four-file tech-design shape is valid only when:\n   519\t\n   520\t- `tech-design.md` exists\n   521\t- `test-plan.md` exists\n   522\t- exactly two additional files matching `tech-design-*.md` exist\n   523\t- `tech-design.md` itself is excluded from the companion count\n   524\t- companion paths are sorted lexically before being returned\n   525\t\n   526\tInvalid examples:\n   527\t\n   528\t- one companion file only\n   529\t- three or more companion files\n   530\t- unrelated files such as `tech-design-notes.md` or `tech-design-archive.md` inside the spec-pack root\n   531\t\n   532\tThese should return `blocked` with an explicit shape error rather than being ignored silently.\n   533\t\n   534\t### TC Mapping for This Flow\n   535\t\n   536\t| TC | Tests | Module | Setup | Assert |\n   537\t|---|---|---|---|---|\n   538\t| TC-1.1a | accepts two-file config | `inspect-command.test.ts` | fixture with `epic.md`, `tech-design.md`, `test-plan.md`, `stories/` | `inspect` returns `ready` and `two-file` |\n   539\t| TC-1.1b | accepts four-file config | `inspect-command.test.ts` | fixture with companions | `inspect` returns `ready` and `four-file` |\n   540\t| TC-1.1c | rejects `stories.md` layout | `inspect-command.test.ts` | invalid story layout fixture | `blocked` with explicit message |\n   541\t| TC-1.1d | rejects missing required artifact | `inspect-command.test.ts` | missing file fixture | `blocked` with named artifact |\n   542\t| TC-1.2a | records two-file config | `inspect-command.test.ts` | two-file fixture | tech-design shape recorded |\n   543\t| TC-1.2b | records four-file config | `inspect-command.test.ts` | four-file fixture | companions surfaced for later use |\n   544\t| TC-1.4a | detects both inserts | `inspect-command.test.ts` | fixture with both insert files | inserts marked `present` |\n   545\t| TC-1.4b | allows absent inserts | `inspect-command.test.ts` | fixture without insert files | inserts marked `absent`, no failure |\n   546\t| TC-1.5a | records full story order | `inspect-command.test.ts` | multi-story fixture | story inventory sorted and returned |\n   547\t| TC-1.6a | preflight carries discovered gates forward | `preflight-command.test.ts` | valid config + discovered gate inputs | readiness report includes gate-aware notes |\n   548\t| TC-1.6b | ambiguous gate policy returns `needs-user-decision` | `preflight-command.test.ts` | missing gate inputs | explicit pause state |\n   549\t| TC-2.1a | primary harness always available check runs | `preflight-command.test.ts` | primary harness fixture | provider matrix includes Claude Code |\n   550\t| TC-2.2a | Codex preferred when configured | `preflight-command.test.ts` | config using Codex | validated config preserves Codex role |\n   551\t| TC-2.2b | Copilot accepted when Codex absent and Copilot chosen | `preflight-command.test.ts` | config using Copilot | provider matrix validates Copilot path |\n   552\t| TC-2.2c | roles using `none` fall back to primary harness only | `preflight-command.test.ts` | config with `none` | no missing-secondary-harness error |\n   553\t| TC-2.3a-e | role model matrix validated | `config-schema.test.ts` | various valid configs | all role assignments parsed correctly |\n   554\t| TC-2.4a | validated run config returned for log capture | `preflight-command.test.ts` | valid fixture | structured `validatedConfig` returned |\n   555\t\n   556\t## Flow 2: Story Implementation and Continuation\n   557\t\n   558\t**Covers:** AC-3.1, AC-3.2, AC-3.4a, AC-4.1 to AC-4.5\n   559\t\n   560\t`story-implement` launches the implementor for one story using the assembled prompt and the configured role assignment. The CLI owns prompt assembly, provider invocation, self-review prompt evolution, and the returned structured result. It does not own story acceptance. `story-continue` exists because the story implementor may keep working in the same provider session across follow-up fixes or post-review continuation.\n   561\t\n   562\tThe most important boundary here is continuity. The CLI may continue a provider session, but it must do so only from explicit continuation/session handles emitted in the previous result and handed back by the orchestrator. The CLI should never try to recover the “latest” session implicitly.\n   563\t\n   564\t```mermaid\n   565\tsequenceDiagram\n   566\t    participant Orchestrator\n   567\t    participant CLI\n   568\t    participant Prompt as Prompt Assembly\n   569\t    participant Adapter as Provider Adapter\n   570\t    participant Harness as Secondary or Primary Harness\n   571\t\n   572\t    Note over Orchestrator,Harness: AC-3.1 to AC-4.5\n   573\t    Orchestrator->>CLI: story-implement --story-id <id>\n   574\t    CLI->>Prompt: Build implementor prompt\n   575\t    Prompt-->>CLI: assembled prompt + manifest\n   576\t    CLI->>Adapter: run implementor role\n   577\t    Adapter->>Harness: execute prompt\n   578\t    Harness-->>Adapter: result + session handle\n   579\t    CLI->>Prompt: run self-review pass 1..N\n   580\t    CLI-->>Orchestrator: structured implementor result + continuation handle\n   581\t```\n   582\t\n   583\t### Continuation Handle Contract\n   584\t\n   585\t```typescript\n   586\texport interface ContinuationHandle {\n   587\t  provider: \"claude-code\" | \"codex\" | \"copilot\";\n   588\t  sessionId: string;\n   589\t  storyId: string;\n   590\t}\n   591\t```\n   592\t\n   593\t### Implementor Result Contract\n   594\t\n   595\t```typescript\n   596\texport interface ImplementorResult {\n   597\t  resultId: string;\n   598\t  provider: \"claude-code\" | \"codex\" | \"copilot\";\n   599\t  model: string;\n   600\t  role: \"story_implementor\";\n   601\t  sessionId?: string;\n   602\t  outcome:\n   603\t    | \"ready-for-verification\"\n   604\t    | \"needs-followup-fix\"\n   605\t    | \"needs-human-ruling\"\n   606\t    | \"blocked\";\n   607\t  continuation?: ContinuationHandle;\n   608\t  story: {\n   609\t    id: string;\n   610\t    title: string;\n   611\t  };\n   612\t  planSummary: string;\n   613\t  changedFiles: Array<{ path: string; reason: string }>;\n   614\t  tests: {\n   615\t    added: string[];\n   616\t    modified: string[];\n   617\t    removed: string[];\n   618\t    totalAfterStory?: number;\n   619\t    deltaFromPriorBaseline?: number;\n   620\t  };\n   621\t  gatesRun: Array<{ command: string; result: \"pass\" | \"fail\" | \"not-run\" }>;\n   622\t  selfReview: {\n   623\t    passesRun: number;\n   624\t    findingsFixed: string[];\n   625\t    findingsSurfaced: string[];\n   626\t  };\n   627\t  openQuestions: string[];\n   628\t  specDeviations: string[];\n   629\t  recommendedNextStep: string;\n   630\t}\n   631\t```\n   632\t\n   633\t### Self-Review Rules\n   634\t\n   635\tThe CLI should own the mechanical shape of self-review:\n   636\t\n   637\t- Pass 1: broad correctness and obvious omissions\n   638\t- Pass 2: test/coverage and requirement alignment\n   639\t- Pass 3: residual risk and boundary audit\n   640\t\n   641\tStop rules:\n   642\t\n   643\t- stop early if the provider reports a blocking condition\n   644\t- stop early if a pass surfaces only human-ruling issues\n   645\t- otherwise run the configured number of passes\n   646\t\n   647\t### TC Mapping for This Flow\n   648\t\n   649\t| TC | Tests | Module | Setup | Assert |\n   650\t|---|---|---|---|---|\n   651\t| TC-3.1a | implementor prompt assembly uses base + snippets | `prompt-assembly.test.ts` | implementor input fixture | assembly includes correct base prompt and snippets |\n   652\t| TC-3.1b | verifier prompt assembly uses base + snippets | `prompt-assembly.test.ts` | verifier input fixture | verifier assets selected correctly |\n   653\t| TC-3.2a | implementor insert applied when present | `prompt-assembly.test.ts` | insert file fixture | insert content included every run |\n   654\t| TC-3.2b | implementor insert omitted cleanly when absent | gate not owned by CLI | `cli-operations-doc.test.ts` | command guide fixture | public docs state gate is orchestrator-owned |\n   783\t\n   784\t## Flow 4: Epic Cleanup, Verification, and Synthesis\n   785\t\n   786\t**Covers:** AC-7.1 to AC-7.3, AC-8.1 to AC-8.4\n   787\t\n   788\tAt epic closeout the CLI executes three different kinds of work: cleanup-only correction, epic-level verifier batches, and synthesis. The orchestrator still determines whether the run can close. The CLI should never blur these responsibilities into one vague “finish the epic” command.\n   789\t\n   790\tThe cleanup operation is distinct because it acts on an orchestrator-curated cleanup batch. It is not a general-purpose retry. It is a bounded correction-only workflow designed to start epic verification from the cleaned state rather than from an accumulation of known-but-deferred issues.\n   791\t\n   792\t```mermaid\n   793\tsequenceDiagram\n   794\t    participant Orchestrator\n   795\t    participant CLI\n   796\t    participant Cleanup as Cleanup Runner\n   797\t    participant EV as Epic Verifier Batch\n   798\t    participant SYN as Epic Synthesizer\n   799\t\n   800\t    Note over Orchestrator,SYN: AC-7.1 to AC-8.4\n   801\t    Orchestrator->>CLI: epic-cleanup --batch <cleanup.md>\n   802\t    CLI->>Cleanup: apply approved cleanup items\n   803\t    Cleanup-->>CLI: cleanup result\n   804\t    CLI-->>Orchestrator: cleanup outcome\n   805\t    Orchestrator->>CLI: epic-verify\n   806\t    CLI->>EV: launch epic verifiers\n   807\t    EV-->>CLI: epic verifier results\n   808\t    CLI-->>Orchestrator: verifier batch\n   809\t    Orchestrator->>CLI: epic-synthesize\n   810\t    CLI->>SYN: verify and
